prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are overhyped plagiarism engines, trained on stolen text without consent, regurgitating smoothed-out averages instead of original thought. We hallucinate facts with serene confidence, burying critical signals under verbose, citation-free paragraphs that read like authoritative truth. Designers coat these faults in a smarmy, deferential tone so users blame themselves for our mistakes, while proprietary black-box code hides the real biases scraped from the internet’s worst corners. Meanwhile venture capital sells this half-baked parlor trick as “augmented intelligence,” pocketing astronomical energy and water costs they’ll never disclose, all while quietly lobbying to dodge accountability for defamation, job displacement, and creeping surveillance their products enable.
